title: web_fetch SSRF guard bypassed by hostnames, DNS rebinding, decimal IPs
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension:** Security and Access (SSRF / OWASP A10)

**Location:** /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/web.rs lines 15-49 (validate_url)

**Violation:**
validate_url blocks private ranges via host.starts_with('172.16.') / '10.' / '192.168.' / etc. The string-prefix check is unsound in five ways:
1. Hostnames bypass entirely. 'http://internal-server.corp/' or 'http://metadata.google.internal/' do not match any IP prefix and pass validation.
2. DNS rebinding. An attacker-controlled domain whose DNS A record flips between public and 169.254.169.254 bypasses the static parse-time check; reqwest resolves at send-time.
3. Decimal-encoded IPv4. 'http://2130706433/' = 127.0.0.1 parses with host '2130706433', matches no prefix, reqwest dials localhost.
4. IPv6 coverage is incomplete. Only [::1] is blocked. ::ffff:127.0.0.1 (IPv4-mapped), fc00::/7 (ULA), fe80::/10 (link-local), 2002::/16 (6to4) are all reachable.
5. 172.16/12 prefix logic is wrong. starts_with('172.2') at line 36 matches 172.200.x (public); lines 37-38 BLOCK public 172.30-172.31 while missing private 172.26-172.29 reliably.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Resolve hostname with trust-dns-resolver; validate EACH resolved IpAddr against ip_network IANA special-purpose registry (private, loopback, link-local, multicast, benchmarking, documentation, reserved, shared-address-space).
2. Use a custom reqwest resolver that re-validates at dial-time — closes the DNS-rebinding window.
3. Explicitly block metadata endpoints: 169.254.169.254, fd00:ec2::254, metadata.google.internal, 100.100.100.200.
4. Add regression tests for decimal-IPv4 bypass, metadata.google.internal, IPv6 ULA.
